vocab.meaning: quan điểm thực dụng (coi trọng giá trị sử dụng)
vocab.example: From a utilitarian perspective, I see no reason to spend a fortune on footwear when basic models serve the same purpose.
I'm often deterred by the hefty price tag associated with luxury sneakers, preferring mid-range alternatives.

vocab.meaning: chi phí trên mỗi lần sử dụng
vocab.example: I justify expensive leather boots by calculating the cost-per-wear, as they tend to last for decades.

vocab.meaning: biểu tượng của địa vị/đẳng cấp
vocab.example: For many of my peers, high-end shoes are a status symbol, but I personally prioritize comfort over prestige.

vocab.meaning: thu nhập khả dụng (tiền dư ra sau khi trừ chi phí thiết yếu)
vocab.example: I only allocate a small fraction of my discretionary income to footwear, as I'd rather spend it on travel.

vocab.meaning: sự mệt mỏi/chán nản với thời trang nhanh
vocab.example: Experiencing fast-fashion fatigue has led me to spend more on a few high-quality pairs rather than dozens of cheap ones.
I refuse to pay extortionate prices for limited-edition releases that are driven solely by artificial hype.

vocab.meaning: sự hỗ trợ chỉnh hình (giúp bảo vệ bàn chân)
vocab.example: I am willing to invest heavily in shoes that offer superior orthopedic support to prevent long-term joint pain.

vocab.meaning: người săn hàng giá rẻ/giảm giá
vocab.example: Being a bit of a bargain hunter, I usually wait for seasonal clearances before purchasing any expensive footwear.
Some collectors spend thousands on shoes, viewing them as assets that might retain or increase their resale value over time.
